description: Israeli organized crime syndicate founded by brothers Yaakov, Avi, and Itzhak Abergil in Ramat Gan, Israel, in the 1990s. Involved in drug trafficking, money laundering, extortion, and murder. Key leaders were arrested and extradited to the United States, with Itzhak Abergil receiving multiple life sentences in 2022.

country_links.evidence_quote: Itzhak Abergil the person responsible for the ecstasy shipments from Belgium and the Netherlands.
country_links.context: The Abergil organization shipped ecstasy from Belgium to the United States.

country_links.evidence_quote: Asi Vaknin, who was arrested in Mexico and brought to the United States in April 2011
country_links.context: Asi Vaknin, associated with the Abergil organization, was arrested in Mexico.

country_links.evidence_quote: Itzhak Abergil the person responsible for the ecstasy shipments from Belgium and the Netherlands.
country_links.context: The Abergil organization shipped ecstasy from the Netherlands to the United States.

country_links.evidence_quote: Itzhak Abergil had offered to help with the slaying during a meeting in Spain
country_links.context: Itzhak Abergil met with associates in Spain to discuss a murder.

country_links.evidence_quote: the crime organization headed by Itzhak and Meir Abergil ran a flourishing money-laundering business in the US from 2002 to 2006... The Abergils, along with Moshe Malul and Israel Ozifa, are also charged with smuggling cocaine and hashish from Europe to the United States.
country_links.context: The Abergil organization ran a money-laundering business and drug trafficking operations in the United States.
